summary: Matched several small wrapper-style functions in Kirby/Yoshi copy ability physics and item code. The PR replaced easy_func placeholders with simple calls/constant returns, adjusted prototypes where return types or parameter names became clear, and added includes needed to expose item helper declarations.
what_changed: Implemented five previously placeholder Kirby/Yoshi special physics callbacks in ftKb_Init.c as direct one-call wrappers around common fighter physics helpers.
ftKb_YsSpecialN1_Phys, ftKb_YsSpecialAirNCapture1_Phys, and ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture1_Phys now call ft_80084F3C(gobj).
ftKb_YsSpecialAirNCapture2_Phys and ftKb_YsSpecialAirCapture1_Phys now call ft_80084EEC(gobj).
Implemented it_2725_Logic86_DmgDealt(Item_GObj* gobj) in itcrazyhandbomb.c as a call to it_802F1344() followed by return 1.
Implemented it_802CC160(Item_GObj* gobj) in itlizardon.c as a call to it_80279FF8(gobj) followed by return 0.
Changed it_802CC160 declaration in itlizardon.h from bool to s32 to match the implementation.
Added a parameter name to the it_2725_Logic86_DmgDealt prototype in itcrazyhandbomb.h.
Added includes for itcrazyhandbomb.h in it_2725.h and it/it_2725.h in itlizardon.c to make helper/prototype dependencies available.
smart_moves: Recognized that several action-state physics callbacks were thin dispatch wrappers to existing shared physics routines rather than needing bespoke logic.
Matched item callbacks by preserving the side-effect call first and returning the exact small integer constant afterward.
Updated header prototypes alongside C implementations, including correcting it_802CC160 to s32 once the function body showed a numeric return.
Kept implementations minimal and idiomatic for easy_funcs: no unnecessary temporaries, no unused gobj access when the matched function does not require it.
decomp_lessons: For small action-state callbacks, first test whether the function is only a wrapper around an existing shared helper such as ft_80084F3C or ft_80084EEC.
Item event callbacks may match as side-effect helper calls followed by constant returns; do not overfit semantics when the assembly only shows a helper call and literal return.
When a matched body returns 0 or 1, verify the intended prototype against generated code and call sites; bool is not always the correct declaration, as shown by it_802CC160 being changed to s32.
Header include dependencies should be fixed at the same time as function matching if the implementation needs declarations from another item module.
Even trivial placeholder replacements are useful progress when they remove named asm stubs and clarify callback table behavior.
naming_conventions: Kirby copy ability functions kept the existing ftKb_YsSpecial... naming pattern for Yoshi special callbacks.
Item functions retained address-style names where semantics were not yet recovered, such as it_802CC160 and it_80279FF8.
Crazy Hand Bomb logic callbacks used the existing it_2725_Logic86_* naming convention.
Header prototypes include concrete parameter names where known, e.g. Item_GObj* gobj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use direct wrapper bodies for functions that only forward Fighter_GObj* to a shared physics helper.
For callbacks that ignore their gobj parameter, leave it unused if the assembly only calls a no-argument helper and returns a constant.
Return literal 1 from bool-like item damage callback it_2725_Logic86_DmgDealt after calling it_802F1344().
Return literal 0 from s32 item function it_802CC160 after calling it_80279FF8(gobj).
Adjust function signatures to the matched return width/type instead of assuming bool from old placeholder declarations.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body or human text excerpt was available, so motivation beyond matching a few easy_funcs is inferred from the title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftKb_YsSpecial physics placeholders that may be direct wrappers around ft_80084F3C or ft_80084EEC.
Audit item callback prototypes near itlizardon and itcrazyhandbomb for bool-versus-s32 mismatches.
Search for remaining it_2725_Logic86_* placeholders that may match as simple helper-call plus constant-return functions.
Check whether it_802CC184, it_802CC1A4, and it_802CC1CC have similar trivial bodies adjacent to it_802CC160.
